mappings.reason: Directly establishes and maintains an enterprise asset inventory with periodic bi-annual review and reconciliation.
Addresses unauthorized assets found during inventory processes but does not establish the inventory itself.
Supports inventory maintenance through active discovery but does not establish the inventory record itself.
Supports inventory updates through DHCP logging but does not establish the inventory itself.
Supports inventory maintenance through passive discovery but does not establish the inventory itself.
Addresses software inventory which is related to system inventory but covers different scope.
Addresses data inventory which is related to asset inventory but different scope.
Addresses asset management but focuses on secure configuration rather than inventory maintenance.
Addresses account inventory which is related to asset inventory but different scope.
Addresses service account inventory which is related to asset inventory but different scope.
Addresses authentication system inventory which is a subset of information systems inventory.
Requires asset knowledge for scanning but does not directly maintain system inventory.
Requires asset knowledge for scanning but does not directly maintain system inventory.
Addresses network infrastructure which is part of information systems inventory.
Architecture diagrams may include system inventory information but not comprehensive.
Requires knowledge of remote assets but focuses on access control rather than inventory.
NAC solutions often discover and track assets but focus on access control.
Addresses service provider inventory which is related to asset inventory but different scope.
